data.title: Palantir CEO Alex Karp Criticizes 'Tokenmaxxing' as AI Costs Rise
data.description:
Alex Karp, the CEO of AI software company Palantir (PLTR), is pushing back against the token-based AI pricing model used by frontier labs. In an interview with CNBC, Karp stated that something has "gone completely wrong" with the way companies are being encouraged to spend money on tokens. His comments come as businesses are becoming more sensitive to rising AI costs, especially as newer models become more expensive and token usage continues to grow.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The issue is that many AI providers charge customers based on token usage, which generally means the amount of text or data processed by an AI model. However, Palantir argues that this can push companies toward more usage rather than better business outcomes. In a post on X, Palantir criticized what it called "tokenmaxxing" by arguing that enterprises should care less about how many tokens they consume and more about whether AI actually improves their operations.

As a result, more companies are looking at alternatives to closed AI systems. For example, some are using open-weight models, which are cheaper and give customers more flexibility. Meanwhile, others are building custom AI models designed for their own data and workflows. This is where Palantir is trying to position itself.

Nvidia and Palantir Announce a Collaboration

Earlier this week, Palantir announced a collaboration with chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) that uses Nvidia's open-source Nemotron models to help government customers build custom AI systems. According to Karp, technical customers want control over their AI infrastructure. He also said that companies and governments should not hand over too much control of their future AI systems to outside model providers.

Is PLTR Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on PLTR stock based on 13 Buys, five Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average PLTR price target of $181 per share implies 44% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Micron (MU), AMD, and Intel (INTC) Gained $2T in Q2; Will the Stellar Rally in Chip Stocks Continue?
data.description:
The second quarter of 2026 closed on a strong note for chip giants Micron Technology (MU), Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), and Intel (INTC). The three stocks gained about $2 trillion in combined market capitalization in the second quarter, according to CNBC. In fact, Micron now ranks among the top ten most valuable U.S. tech companies.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

NVDS: built for a short position on NVDA

Notably, Micron stock rallied more than 240%, while AMD and Intel stocks surged 186% and 216%, respectively, in Q2 2026. In comparison, Nvidia (NVDA) stock rose only 15% in Q2 2026. Investor interest in Nvidia's competitors has increased in recent months. Meanwhile, the Magnificent 7 stocks have been under pressure recently and collectively lost $2.3 trillion in just June amid concerns about massive capital spending on AI infrastructure.

While Micron, AMD, and Intel stocks plunged significantly on July 1, they are still up significantly year-to-date. Using TipRanks' Stock Comparison Tool, let's look at Wall Street's ratings on Micron, AMD, and Intel and discuss analysts' views on these chip stocks.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Tesla (TSLA) Retail Traders May Get Fired Up as Stock Crosses $420
data.description:
Meme levels can actually influence trading. In fact, according to Vanda Research, retail investors tend to buy more shares of EV maker Tesla (TSLA) when the price rises above $420 per share. More specifically, five-day retail net buying increases on average from $87 million to $135 million once Tesla crosses above that level, according to Vanda global macro strategist Viraj Patel.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

Explore TSLQ for 2X short leverage on TSLA

It's worth noting that Tesla has one of the most active retail investor bases in the market, and as a result, certain price levels can become self-reinforcing when enough traders pay attention to them.

Why Do Tesla Investors Buy at $420?

The reason why Tesla investors buy shares at $420 is because of Elon Musk. The number is widely linked to cannabis culture, and over time, it has turned into a running joke among Musk supporters and meme-stock traders. On Tuesday, Tesla closed at $420.60, which is notable because it marked the first time in its 16 years as a public company that its closing price actually included 420. Naturally, this quickly led to jokes across X.

Furthermore, this level also brings back memories of Musk's well-known 2018 tweet, in which he said he was considering taking Tesla private at $420 with "funding secured." While that comment initially pushed the stock higher, it later resulted in a securities fraud case from the SEC. However, it is important to mention that the move was not driven by memes alone.

Indeed, Tesla has also been recovering with the overall market, and investors are waiting for the company's second-quarter delivery report. In addition, Roundhill Investments CEO Dave Mazza told Bloomberg that the technical setup looks stronger, as momentum continues to improve. Interestingly, though, Tesla's retail flows were actually lower on Wednesday, which suggests that the hype hasn't begun yet. Nevertheless, investors bought more Tesla stock in nine out of 14 instances when shares crossed above $420 since mid-2024.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 11 Buys, 15 Holds, and three Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $403.49 per share implies 5.1% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Bloom Energy Stock (BE) Price Target Raised to a New Street-High – Here's Why
data.description:
Solid oxide fuel cell system developer Bloom Energy (BE) has received a new street-high price target today. UBS analyst Manav Gupta lifted his price target to $350 (20.5% upside) from $322, while reiterating a Buy rating on BE stock. The five-star analyst cited stronger demand for Bloom's fuel cells and an expanded partnership with Brookfield Asset Management (BAM).

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

Two ETFs to trade BE long or short: BEX & BEZ

The analyst said the new deal marks a turning point for Bloom's role in powering AI infrastructure. Bloom and Brookfield first set up a $5 billion partnership in October 2025 to supply onsite power for Brookfield's planned AI factories. That agreement has now been expanded to $25 billion. The increased funding will help Bloom scale its fuel cell systems globally and deepen its position as a preferred onsite power provider for Brookfield's AI buildouts.

Gupta said the companies are advancing a new model for AI factories that ties together power, compute, data center design, and capital from day one, giving Bloom a long runway for growth.

Currently, Gupta ranks 343 out of more than 12,300 analysts tracked by TipRanks. He has a success rate of 82% on BE stock, with an average return per rating of 266.87% over a one-year timeframe.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Lose Steam as AI Stocks Face Pressure ahead of Key Jobs Data
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) finished in negative territory on Wednesday as traders sold off AI-related stocks.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • In his first public appearance since the June interest rate decision, Fed Chair Kevin Warsh noted that inflation risks have eased. "Expectations of inflation over the first four weeks of this period have come down, inflation risks have come down," he said at the European Central Bank (ECB) forum in Sintra, Portugal.
  • Rate hike odds trended lower following his comments. The odds of a 25 bps hike at the July 29 Fed meeting on the CME FedWatch tool dropped to 27.3% from 33.1% a day ago. In addition, the odds of two hikes by the September 16 meeting fell to 13.5% from 17.2%.
  • Elsewhere, ADP said private employers added a net 98,000 jobs in June, falling short of the consensus estimate of 118,000 additions. Education and health services accounted for nearly half of the gains at 48,000. On the other hand, natural resources and mining was the only negative sector with a loss of 5,000 jobs.
  • The data provides a preview to June's jobs report, which the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) will publish on Thursday at 8:30 a.m. Eastern Time. The report includes nonfarm payrolls and the unemployment rate.
  • AI-related stocks, including Micron (MU), AMD (AMD), and SanDisk (SNDK), faced pressure as investors rotated into beaten-down software names. These include Palantir (PLTR), Salesforce (CRM), and Adobe (ADBE).
  • Meta Platforms (META) popped after announcing a plan to sell excess compute.
  • Robinhood (HOOD) rallied after holding its "The World is Flat" crypto event. The brokerage introduced Robinhood Chain, a public blockchain for tokenized stock products.
  • ServiceNow (NOW) surged after Guggenheim analyst John DiFucci issued a price target of $125 and upgraded NOW to "Buy" from "Neutral."
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: CNN Shakeup as Paula Reid is Expected to Depart, Paramount Skydance (NASDAQ:PSKY) Stock Gains
data.description:
One of the biggest points of concern in the merger between entertainment giant Paramount Skydance (PSKY) and Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) is what happens to CNN. Since Paramount already controls CBS News, the idea that CNN may be under the same umbrella is a little too much for some to stomach. Reports note that it may already be too much for one correspondent to stomach as Paula Reid is set to depart before a Paramount era can kick in. Investors took the news oddly well, and gave Paramount shares a nearly 3% boost in the closing minutes of Wednesday's trading.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Paula Reid is currently CNN's chief legal affairs correspondent, reports note, and that Reid was offered the choice to renew her contract, but decided not to do so. Reid departed thanks to "…uncertainty of continuing to work at CNN as it faces a new future under different corporate management."

This, naturally, has raised further questions within CNN, which was already under some strain as it began to consider a possible future under Paramount. Meanwhile, Reid may be planning to move directly to CBS News herself, and likely has at least a few contacts therein to help smooth the process. There is still plenty of uncertainty around the merger process, even as it clears multiple legal hurdles, so continuing to keep an eye on the situation will likely prove worthwhile.

Shutting One Part Down

A part of the merger arrangement many expected to happen, meanwhile, is going official. Paramount plans to shut down its joint venture with Universal (CMCSA), which was said to be part of the required remedy slate from the European Union to get the deal to pass.

The joint venture, known as UIP, got its start all the way back in 1981. These days, it mostly serves as a European distributor, handling Universal films for theatrical release therein. Shutting this operation down should clear the way for EU approval, though there remains concern in Great Britain as well as two state governments in the United States left to address. The British concern, reports noted, was over a perhaps-unduly concentrated number of people handling United Kingdom media.

Is Paramount Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on PSKY stock based on one Buy, four Holds and three Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 23.21% loss in its share price over the past year, the average PSKY price target of $11.33 per share implies 11.38% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Robinhood (HOOD) Brings Crypto-Style 24/7 Trading to Commodities and Currencies, Stock Surges
data.description:
Investing platform Robinhood (HOOD) is doubling down on 24/7 trading by launching perpetual futures for customers in Europe. These contracts, often called "perps," have no expiration date and allow traders to maintain leveraged positions around the clock. Robinhood said that the new products will be linked to assets such as gold (GLD), silver (SLV), crude oil (USO), and the euro-dollar exchange rate (USD-EUR). In addition, investors can use up to 10x leverage. Interestingly, shares surged on the news.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The move is part of Robinhood's strategy to bring crypto-style market access into mainstream finance. In fact, perpetual futures have been common in crypto for years because digital assets trade nonstop, unlike stocks that often depend on traditional market hours. However, recent global events have made some traders want around-the-clock access to their investments.

For example, during the early weeks of the Iran conflict, traders used decentralized platforms like Hyperliquid to bet on oil and other assets over the weekend while major markets were closed. It's worth noting that Robinhood has already seen strong demand for this type of product. The company launched crypto perpetual futures in Europe last year, and they have since become one of its fastest-growing offerings.

Robinhood Announces New Crypto-Related Products

Separately, Robinhood recently announced new crypto-related products at its London event. These include a decentralized lending product and AI trading accounts for U.S. crypto investors. The company also introduced Crypto Earn, which lets U.S. users lend the USDG stablecoin through a self-custody wallet for an estimated 7% annual yield. Meanwhile, Robinhood said it plans to launch a crypto trading platform in the U.K. soon.

Is HOOD Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on HOOD stock based on 16 Buys, three Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average HOOD price target of $104.59 per share implies 3.8% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: 'Look Beyond the Noise,' Says Investor as Microsoft Stock Sits at a Crossroads
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) has struggled to find its footing this year, with the stock still down 22% year-to-date. Concerns have piled up, ranging from soaring AI capital spending and heavy reliance on its OpenAI partnership to fears of a looming "SaaSpocalypse."

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The biggest question mark is capital spending. Microsoft expects to pour about $190 billion into capital expenditures this year. While that's broadly in line with some of its hyperscale rivals, a significant portion of that investment is going toward relatively short-lived assets such as GPUs and CPUs, raising questions about how often those assets will need to be refreshed and whether the returns will justify the spending.

Then there's OpenAI. Microsoft's commercial remaining performance obligation (RPO) has climbed to an impressive $627 billion, up 99% from a year ago. However, bears are quick to point out that roughly 45% of that backlog is tied to OpenAI, a company that, despite its explosive growth, has yet to turn a profit.

Another concern making the rounds is the prospect of a "SaaSpocalypse" – the idea that AI agents and capable foundation models could upend the traditional software-as-a-service business model. The bearish argument is that AI could reduce demand for standalone software applications by allowing users to complete many tasks directly through AI assistants.

Yet, one investor, known by the pseudonym Quad 7 Capital, thinks all the fretting is misplaced.

"To us the recent action is an overreaction where short-term sentiment is outweighing the long-term structural dominance of this great American company," states the 5-star investor.

Quad 7 argues that Microsoft is delivering on both the top and bottom lines, posting a "massive earnings beat" in fiscal Q3. Beyond total sales of $82.9 billion (a jump of 18% year-over-year), Microsoft Cloud's revenue rose by 29% to reach $54.5 billion.

When it comes to OpenAI, Quad 7 views Microsoft's decision to remove exclusivity clauses from the partnership as a positive step. The investor also points out that, as a major stakeholder, Microsoft stands to reap a significant windfall if OpenAI eventually goes public.

In addition, Quad 7 appreciates the company's moves vis-a-vis Y Combinator, offering AI startups $150,000 in Azure credits. The investor likes this strategy, believing it will help lock in future revenues by cultivating ongoing relationships between startups and the Microsoft ecosystem.

"Microsoft is paying for the up-front infrastructure costs to guarantee it capture(s) these companies as high-margin, paying customers down the road," adds Quad 7.

Therefore, this year's dip isn't a sign of more trouble on the horizon, according to Quad 7. On the contrary, for those willing to look beyond the noise, this is a great chance to buy a long-term winner before it starts to climb back up again.

 "We think at this valuation, Microsoft sets up as an interesting investment opportunity," concludes Quad 7, who rates MSFT a Buy. (To watch Quad 7's track record, click here)

Wall Street is also feeling good about Microsoft. With 35 Buys and just one Hold, MSFT soars to a Strong Buy consensus rating. Its 12-month average price target of $562.10 implies about 51% upside from current levels. (See MSFT stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Mixed After Strong Quarter Close
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were mixed on Tuesday evening after a strong regular trading session that marked the conclusion of the second quarter and first half of the year. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 Index (SPX) were up 0.03% and 0.02%, respectively, at 6:37 p.m. EDT on June 30, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) futures were down 0.13%.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

In the regular session, the Nasdaq, the S&P 500, and the Dow rose 1.5%, 0.8%, and 0.3%, respectively, on Tuesday. For the quarter, the S&P climbed 15% and the Nasdaq jumped 21%, their strongest gains since Q2 2020. The Dow rose 13%, just shy of its 15% advance in late 2022. The performance of the Russell 2000 stood out, up 22%, reflecting its best first half since 1991.

Chip and AI stocks have powered much of the market's run, with a record rally adding $2 trillion in value to Micron (MU), Intel (INTC), and AMD (AMD) in Q2.

On the economic front, traders now look ahead to Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's remarks on Wednesday, along with fresh data on jobs and manufacturing.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi
